title: Lesson Quiz: Evaluating Career Choices
description: Assessment of the concept that Comparing options and making informed decisions.

questions.prompt: What does it mean to evaluate career choices?

choices.text: To compare career options carefully using evidence
choices.feedback: Correct. Evaluating career choices means comparing options carefully and using evidence to support decisions.

choices.text: To choose the first option that sounds interesting
choices.feedback: Evaluating choices involves comparison, not a quick first impression.

choices.text: To ignore all research once a career seems appealing
choices.feedback: Research should still be used when evaluating a career option.
Evaluating career choices should be based on the student's own comparison and evidence.

questions.prompt: An informed career decision should be supported by facts and comparison.

choices.feedback: Informed decisions should be based on evidence and comparison.
Correct. Informed decisions are stronger when students compare options and use facts.

questions.prompt: Which factor belongs in evaluating two career options?

choices.text: Comparing requirements, duties, and personal fit
choices.feedback: This idea is correct, but the best answer in this set is the one that more directly highlights use of multiple evidence categories.

choices.text: Using only one career website and no other information
choices.feedback: Evaluating choices should include more than one kind of information.

choices.text: Looking at more than one piece of evidence before deciding
choices.feedback: Correct. Career evaluation should include multiple pieces of evidence.

choices.text: Avoiding comparison so the decision feels easier
choices.feedback: Comparison is an important part of evaluation.

questions.prompt: Why is comparing more than one career option useful?

choices.text: It helps students make more thoughtful decisions using evidence
choices.feedback: This idea is correct, but the strongest answer in this set is the one that more directly explains how comparison supports decision quality.

choices.text: It guarantees the perfect career choice every time
choices.feedback: Comparison helps, but it does not guarantee perfection.

choices.text: It removes the need for self-reflection
choices.feedback: Self-reflection still matters when comparing careers.

choices.text: It helps students judge strengths and limits of options before choosing
choices.feedback: Correct. Comparing more than one option helps students understand differences before deciding.

questions.prompt: Evaluating career choices can include both research evidence and personal reflection.

choices.feedback: Correct. Good career evaluation includes both research evidence and personal reflection.
Research and personal reflection both matter in evaluating career choices.
